name: Quesadillas
recipeInstructions:
1. Dampen the edge of the tortilla slightly.
2. Place a tablespoonful of filling on one half of the tortilla, away from the edge.
3. Press the other half of the tortilla over the filling, pressing the edges together well.
4. Grease a comal, griddle, or skillet very lightly.
5. Cook the quesadillas on both sides and then cook the broad, curved part until well cooked on the outside with some dark brown patches on the dough.
6. Do not get the comal too hot; otherwise, the quesadillas will burn on the outside while staying raw inside.
7. Serve immediately. They become leathery if left to stand.
Filling: Use strips of roasted, peeled Poblano peppers and Asadero or Oaxaca cheese. Chopped boiled potatoes mixed with Mexican chorizo (do not substitute Spanish or Portuguese) is also very good.
